description:
## Background and intent

The earlier beads can all pass in isolation while the actual user path still fails: Rust may parse fixtures, TauRPC may compile, Effect may map states, and React may render, but the desktop app might still not show real Beadwork issues when launched from a workspace. This final bead is the user-level proof for the first Issue List slice.

The goal is not a giant test framework. The goal is a repeatable smoke/e2e script with useful logging that a future agent can run before closing the epic. It should make failures diagnosable: which workspace was used, whether `bw` was found, what command path was exercised, and what UI state appeared.

## What to build

Add an end-to-end or smoke-test path that launches or exercises Beadsmith against a small deterministic Beadwork workspace and verifies the issue explorer renders real IssueSummary data through the full Rust adapter -> TauRPC -> Effect -> React path. Prefer the lightest reliable setup that works in this Tauri/Vite repo. If full desktop automation is too heavy for this slice, document that trade-off and provide the strongest practical smoke script plus a clear manual verification checklist.

The test fixture should include at least one issue with labels/dependencies or optional metadata, and one scenario that produces a true empty list or a controlled read failure if practical. Logs should identify the workspace path, command/result state, and UI state being asserted.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A repeatable smoke/e2e command or script exists for the completed Issue List slice.
- [ ] The script verifies that at least one real Beadwork issue appears in the issue explorer when launched against a deterministic workspace.
- [ ] The verification path exercises the approved integration stack: Rust adapter, TauRPC boundary, Effect service, and React rendering.
- [ ] The script emits detailed logs for workspace setup, `bw` availability/path, app launch or service invocation, and assertions.
- [ ] Empty or failure-state coverage is included if practical; if not practical, the closing comment documents why and what should cover it later.
- [ ] The smoke command is documented where future agents can find it.
- [ ] The test does not require committing local machine-specific paths.
- [ ] Relevant checks plus the new smoke/e2e command pass before closing the epic.

## Out of scope

- Full regression coverage for future workspace switching, search, filters, issue detail, editing, or mutations.
- Replacing focused unit tests from earlier beads.

## Triage

ready-for-agent

comments.text: Planning audit: added because isolated unit/contract/render tests can all pass while the actual desktop user path still fails. This final bead should prove the complete adapter -> TauRPC -> Effect -> React flow against a deterministic Beadwork workspace with useful logs for future debugging.

title: Add end-to-end smoke coverage for the Issue List slice
